title: The Terror of History
subtitle: On Eliade, meaning, and why suffering needs a meaning.
excerpt: When suffering loses its meaning, it becomes unbearable. Eliade called it the "terror of history." Meaning has to be built, not inherited.

title: You Have Never Suffered From Anything But Love
excerpt: Every grief, every breakup, every quiet midnight: the same suffering wearing different masks. It's always love.
